runs.analysis_summary: crypto_treasury_arb_momentum — mNAV-driven treasury arbitrage + momentum filters. Current configured watchlist: [DFDV, SBET, BMNR, BTBT, MARA, RIOT, HUT, CIFR, CLSK, CORZ]. Key config snapshot: nav_threshold_pct=0.05, pricing_lag_threshold_pct=0.02, beta_min=1.2, crypto_rally_daily_gain=0.03, risk_per_trade_var95=0.025, max_new_trades_per_run=4, high_beta_mode=false. Underlying asset map links most names to BTC-USD; SBET->ETH-USD.
trade_decisions: No live or paper trades executed — backtest failed and optimizer produced no tradable signals.

Recommended immediate actions (ranked): 1) Seed NAV/treasury holdings for each company (upsert_treasury_holding_tool). 2) Validate OHLCV history for each ticker and replace missing tickers with liquid proxies (COIN, MSTR, GBTC historical, MARA, RIOT). 3) Re-run the walk-forward backtest after data seeding. 4) Add a minimum-trade-count constraint to optimizer and temporarily loosen pricing_lag_threshold_pct to 0.03–0.05 and beta_min to 1.05–1.1 to surface candidate signals for validation.
executed_orders: No broker orders placed (no Alpaca orders submitted).
Operational actions executed: three Discord report messages sent (REPORT PART 1/3, PART 2/3, PART 3/3) summarizing findings and recommendations.
risk_notes: Primary risk: missing or insufficient historical OHLCV and NAV/holdings input — prevents valid backtest and optimization.
Zero-trade optimizer output is a red flag: either filters are too strict, or data/NAV holes suppressed signals — could cause false confidence when fixed.
Small universe leads to high parameter-tuning variance and increases overfitting risk — need more liquid proxies or longer history.
If parameters are aggressively adjusted without ensuring minimum event counts, model will overfit to idiosyncratic events.
Operational risk: incorrect ticker mappings (OTC/legacy tickers) and missing shares_outstanding will break NAV calculations.

runs.objective: Analyze current portfolio (Circle / CRCL, Coinbase / COIN, Spotify / SPOT) with heavy concentration in Circle; identify vulnerabilities; run a 5‑year backtest or a best-effort simulation; recommend risk‑balancing actions. Do NOT change any Alpaca positions (user request).
runs.market_regime: Late-cycle / risk-on-to-mixed. Macro noise and episodic crypto/regulatory shocks remain the dominant tail risk for crypto‑linked equities. Short-term model outputs show weak/negative 1‑day signals for all three names; volatilities are high, especially for CRCL and COIN.
runs.analysis_summary: Hybrid trend + mean-reversion model (tool outputs): per-name next‑day metrics used as short-term inputs; longer-horizon scenario simulations use assumed returns + empirical model volatilities. Note: automated 5‑year long-backtest tool failed for the exact CRCL+COIN+SPOT universe due to insufficient historical history for the exact requested universe (CRCL listing / history constraint). I used available per‑ticker model metrics (vol, short-term bias) and scenario math to produce a transparent 5‑year scenario/backtest approximation given your stated weight tilt toward CRCL.
trade_decisions: No Alpaca edits or orders will be placed (per user instruction). All recommendations below are advisory only.

If you want to lower portfolio risk, consider: reduce absolute allocation to CRCL (target max 25–35% per single-name cap), add low-volatility allocation (cash/T-bills / short-duration bonds), and introduce explicit hedges for crypto exposure before acting.
Implement risk controls: per-position cap (25% max), overall portfolio annualized vol target (e.g., 30–40%), monthly rebalancing, trailing-stop or option-based downside hedge on largest position.
risk_notes: Concentration risk: a 60% weight in CRCL creates idiosyncratic single-name risk. Circle is a company whose equity is tightly coupled to crypto/regulatory risk (USDC reserves/regulatory/legal outcomes).
Market / correlation risk: CRCL and COIN are likely to be highly correlated to broad crypto moves; a crypto crash would heavily impact both names simultaneously, reducing diversification benefits.

Volatility: per-tool annualized vol estimates are very high (CRCL ~142%, COIN ~107%, SPOT ~74%). The portfolio (60/25/15) implies a high overall annual vol (~111% under correlated assumptions used below).
Liquidity and execution: CRCL may have lower free float / higher intraday spreads vs COIN and SPOT; large exits/rebalances could move the market.
Regulatory / sector risk: crypto companies face regulatory, custody, reserve, and AML risks. Circle (issuer of USDC) has additional counterparty / reserve disclosure risk.
Tail risk: the portfolio is exposed to extreme tail moves; stress scenarios can easily produce >50% drawdowns (see scenario results).

Model uncertainty: the automated long-backtest tool could not run the exact 5‑year historical test for the given universe because of insufficient precise history for CRCL; scenario/backtest below is therefore an illustrative simulation with explicit assumptions.

runs.objective: Full analysis of current portfolio (positions: CRCL / COIN / SPOT) with vulnerabilities, a 1‑year backtest/estimate (user-specified: most weight in CIRCLE), risk-balancing suggestions. Do NOT change Alpaca positions. Send report to Discord.
runs.market_regime: Risk-on, crypto‑sensitive environment. Elevated realized volatility across crypto-adjacent equities with strong correlation to Bitcoin and macro risk appetite; rate sensitivity remains a background driver.
runs.analysis_summary: Hybrid trend + mean-reversion next-day model + historical daily price series. Limitations: CRCL trading history is truncated in our data source which prevents a fully reliable walk-forward 1‑year backtest; backtest/results below are conservative estimates that incorporate available CRCL tail data and full 1y series for COIN and SPOT.
trade_decisions: No Alpaca positions will be edited per instruction (NO trades executed).
Recommended portfolio target (example) to reduce tail risk given 'most weight in CIRCLE' requirement: CRCL 40%, COIN 25%, SPOT 15%, Cash/T‑bills 10% (BIL/SHV), Defensive ETF 10%.

Immediate risk-reduction steps to consider manually: trim CRCL from 60%→40% and allocate proceeds to short-term Treasuries / cash; consider buying protective puts on CRCL/COIN or a small short of COIN to reduce crypto beta by ~25–40%.
Position sizing rule: volatility-equalize weights (dollars ∝ 1 / annualized vol) to normalize single-name vol contributions; cap single-name exposure at 40%.

Operational rules: monthly rebalance or rebalance when allocation drifts >5pp; set portfolio trailing reduction trigger if portfolio falls >15% from peak; set hard single-name stop or protective option if CRCL falls >25% from cost.
executed_orders: None — explicit instruction honored (no Alpaca orders placed).
risk_notes: Concentration risk: CRCL at majority weight concentrates idiosyncratic and regulatory exposure into one issuer — single-name failure would heavily damage portfolio.
High correlation and volatility: CRCL and COIN are crypto‑sensitive and likely to fall together when crypto sells off, amplifying drawdowns.
Liquidity & execution risk: CRCL shows episodes of thin liquidity and large intraday ranges — large exits can be costly.
Short-term model risks: all three show short-horizon negative predicted moves; SPOT flagged SELL short-term — near-term downside risk present.
Event risk: regulatory actions, exchange outages, or adverse corporate announcements can produce >30% moves quickly.
Diversification gap: all names are growth/consumer/crypto-adjacent — weak hedge vs rates or broad equity drawdowns.

runs.objective: Full portfolio risk & one-year forward price analysis for a three-asset portfolio (COIN, CRCL, TSLA). Provide vulnerabilities, hedging recommendations, position sizing guidance, and send the full report to Discord.
runs.market_regime: Mixed: Crypto-sensitive regime for COIN/CRCL (high volatility, tail regulatory risk). Macro/tech regime for TSLA (moderate volatility, sensitive to rates, EV demand, and AI/autonomy sentiment). Current environment implies elevated crypto idiosyncratic risk and a medium-risk equity backdrop.
runs.analysis_summary: Hybrid trend + mean-reversion model (per-name next-day metrics) + 2-year daily history from Yahoo Finance for context. Key model outputs used: predicted_1d_return, confidence, annualized volatility, 20d & 60d momentum, model stop-loss and take-profit levels, and signal (HOLD/BUY/SELL).
trade_decisions: Reduce crypto concentration: Trim exposures to COIN and CRCL to target combined crypto exposure of ~25–35% of portfolio (from an assumed equal-weight starting point where crypto is 66%). Reallocate proceeds to cash or lower-vol assets (bonds/ETF) to lower portfolio-level volatility.

Hedge CRCL aggressively: Buy 6–12 month put protection 25–40% OTM sized to cover 50–75% of CRCL notional due to regulatory/stablecoin tail risk. If options are illiquid, use short futures or inverse instruments as a proxy.
Hedge COIN selectively: Buy 3–6 month puts 30–40% OTM sized to cover 25–50% of COIN exposure. Consider cheaper collars (sell near-term calls) to finance the puts if premium cost is high.

TSLA: Partial hedge or take-profit discipline. Buy 3–6 month 20–30% OTM puts sized to cover 20–50% of TSLA exposure or implement collars (sell calls to finance puts). Consider increasing long exposure only if conviction and diversification are improved.

Portfolio-level hedge: Buy index protection (e.g., put spread on QQQ or SPY) sized to offset ~30–50% of portfolio delta during risk-off events. Alternatively, buy BTC puts or short BTC futures to hedge crypto systemic risk directly.

Tactical relative-value: Consider long TSLA / short COIN pair (dollar neutral) to reduce crypto beta while retaining equity upside — scale to target beta neutrality rather than dollar neutrality if you have beta estimates.
Risk controls: Set monitored model stop-loss alerts at model levels (COIN ~$183.8, CRCL ~$102.9, TSLA ~$379.3). Prefer options hedges over hard stops to avoid gapping risk.
risk_notes: Concentration risk: Two-thirds exposure to crypto-driven companies leaves the portfolio highly exposed to a single systemic factor (BTC/crypto market sentiment).

Regulatory tail risk: CRCL is particularly vulnerable to adverse regulatory actions or reserve/transparency issues. COIN is vulnerable to SEC enforcement, exchange-specific rulings, and margin/trading-volume shocks.
Liquidity & option cost risk: Hedging costs can rise during volatility spikes (exactly when protection is needed). Thin options liquidity for CRCL could make puts expensive or difficult to size.
Volatility skew & gap risk: Crypto-related equities can gap lower on overnight news; hard stop-losses can be ineffective. Use options where possible.
Scenario tail risk: Stress scenario example — crypto crash (COIN -70%, CRCL -90%) with TSLA -40% would imply an approximate portfolio loss of ~-66.7% under equal weights.

Model limitations: Daily next-day predictions have low confidence for some names (CRCL confidence 5.5%, COIN 28.6%). Use multi-horizon and scenario analysis; do not treat single-day model output as deterministic.

runs.objective: Run multi-timeframe technical analysis on watchlist and produce actionable short-list + send report to Discord.
runs.market_regime: Mixed — short-term momentum in select names (CRCL, SPOT) while several tickers show weekly weakness or consolidation (COIN, BABA, NRG). Divergence between daily and weekly signals is common; prefer waiting for weekly confirmation before scaling.
runs.analysis_summary: Multi-timeframe technical stack (Ichimoku D/W, MACD D/W slope & histogram, RSI D/W, Bollinger band expansion, Fibonacci swing levels, candle patterns) supplied by technical_watchlist_analysis_tool.
trade_decisions: CRCL — Do NOT initiate new long (FOMO). Wait for RSI cooling or retrace toward 0.886 fib ~78 or daily MACD consolidation. If entering, keep very small size and 1% risk cap.
SPOT — No new initiation. If looking to enter, wait for weekly confirmation or pullback to mid-band (~491) / 0.886 retrace ~448.
XLE — Do not chase; consider add-on weakness near daily mid-band (~55) or 0.886 retrace ~43.23. Use sector-sized position sizing (max exposure 3–5% of portfolio).
COIN — Avoid long; consider range/short bias while weekly remains below cloud. If shorting, use tight risk controls and small size.
RKLB — Valid small-size watchlist long on pullback into support (0.886 retrace ~36). Position size small — treat as watch/trial trade until daily confirms.
AMD — Hold/observe. Consider small add only on clear daily follow-through after bullish cross confirmation and Ichimoku alignment.
WGMI — Bias to short or avoid long. If shorting, use defined stops above recent weekly cloud/top.
NRG — Do not chase. Consider entry only after MACD/R SI recovery or close above weekly cloud base; oversold but weekly risk remains.
DRNZ — No trade (insufficient history). Monitor daily momentum only; treat any entries as speculative small bets.
BABA — FOMO — avoid initiating new longs. Wait for weekly stabilization and RSI recovery; if trading, size very small and use wide stop-loss until weekly confirms.
risk_notes: Avoid chasing names flagged as FOMO (CRCL, SPOT, BABA).
Per-trade risk budget: target 0.5%–1.0% of portfolio risk; max exposure per new position 2.5%–5% depending on conviction.
Use stop-loss near recent swing low (for longs) or swing high (for shorts); prefer hard exits rather than averaging into momentum-driven moves.
Require weekly confirmation for new medium-size positions when daily/week signals diverge.
Thin-history tickers (DRNZ) should be treated as higher data risk and sized accordingly.

runs.objective: Run a 2-hour Alpaca paper session for the crypto_treasury_arb_momentum strategy with full data diagnostics, log all signal evaluations/entries/exits/rejections, enforce risk controls (but avoid zero‑trade mode via adaptive relaxation), and produce a full strategy audit and code-level feedback delivered to Discord.
runs.market_regime: Crypto/crypto‑treasury equities: short-term neutral-to-mildly‑bearish crypto price action with low-to-moderate intraday volatility; sector mNAV center ~7.7. Technicals mixed across the universe (several neutral / a few bearish).
runs.analysis_summary: Hybrid mNAV-driven treasury arbitrage + short-horizon trend/mean-reversion model. Primary inputs: computed mNAV (market_cap / estimated_NAV), 1-day predicted returns from a hybrid trend+mean-reversion model (predicted_1d_return, confidence), multi-timeframe technical stack (Ichimoku, MACD, RSI, Bollinger), liquidity filters, and risk guardrails (single_name_max, sector_max, risk_per_trade_var95).
trade_decisions: No new trades placed during the 120-minute session. System evaluated the universe each minute (117 cycles) and adapted thresholds at the 30-minute mark but still found no candidates meeting combined data + signal + risk criteria.

Adaptive relaxation executed: deviation_threshold lowered per adaptive policy (30-min trigger) but no additional candidates passed the relaxed threshold and other risk checks (liquidity, single_name_max, model confidence).
Existing positions were retained (no automated exits triggered).
executed_orders: No new paper orders placed by the session (total_orders_placed=0).

Carried existing paper positions (no fills during session): CEPO long 572 @ avg 10.49; COIN short 16 @ avg 181.5943; HUT long 60 @ avg 49.85; MARA short 335 @ avg 8.8051; MSTR long 22 @ avg 135.5314; XXI long 956 @ avg 6.27499.
risk_notes: Primary data dependency risk: missing treasury holdings entries for 6/10 universe symbols (DFDV, SBET, BMNR, BTBT, CIFR, CORZ) prevented mNAV computation and reduced tradable universe by >50%.
Strict mNAV deviation threshold (0.15) plus model confidence cutoff resulted in zero new entries over 2 hours; optimizer expectation (1–3 trades/day) not met.
NAV calculation vulnerability: relies on accurate shares_outstanding and up-to-date crypto prices. CLSK had incomplete NAV/market cap inputs.

Exposure controls (single_name_max=20%, portfolio_sector_max=60%) functioning correctly and prevented borderline entries when signals were marginal — good for risk control but contributes to undertrading when data is sparse.
Operational vulnerability: reference site fetch failure for at least one source (TheBlock returned 403), reducing cross-check redundancy.

runs.objective: Run a 15-minute autonomous Alpaca paper session for the crypto-treasury strategy: refresh holdings at startup, use the live tradable watchlist only, enforce mNAV and exposure risk controls, allow adaptive threshold relaxation if triggered, and only execute high-conviction setups. Provide per-symbol evaluation logs and a final Discord report.
runs.market_regime: Intraday: low mNAV dispersion across treasury names, market open and liquid. Short-horizon momentum signals weak; no symbols breached the configured mNAV deviation gate (15%).
runs.analysis_summary: mNAV-driven treasury-arbitrage + short-horizon momentum/mean-reversion overlay. Key rules: compute mNAV = market_cap / estimated_NAV (NAV ≈ crypto holdings + cash - debt); require absolute mNAV deviation >= 15% vs peer baseline to pass primary gate; require predicted_1d_return and model confidence above high-conviction cutoffs; enforce per-symbol exposure caps and minimum $/leg sizing to avoid economically trivial trades; adaptive deviation relaxation allowed but only if multiple high-conviction candidates clustered and liquidity adequate.
trade_decisions: Session duration: 15 minutes (2026-03-16T16:12:56Z to 2026-03-16T16:28:06Z).
Cycles run: 14 (poll interval 60s). Deviation threshold used: 15%. Adaptive relaxation allowed but NOT applied.
Candidate universe evaluated: MSTR, MARA, RIOT, COIN, HUT, CLSK, TSLA (live tradable watchlist).
Orders placed: 0 (no trades executed). Remaining trade budget after session: 10.

Primary reasons for no trades: no symbol passed the mNAV deviation gate; existing per-symbol exposures blocked increases; predicted 1‑day returns / model confidence below high-conviction cutoff; minimum $/leg constraint made potential trades uneconomic.
risk_notes: Top blockers preventing trades: (1) No symbols breached configured mNAV deviation threshold (15%) — primary gate. (2) Existing position/exposure caps for MARA, COIN, HUT prevented increasing exposure. (3) Model predicted short-horizon returns and confidence were below high-conviction cutoffs for all candidates. (4) Minimum economic trade sizing rule rejected small edge opportunities where execution cost would dominate expected return. (5) Adaptive relaxation was permitted by policy but not triggered because there were no clustered high-conviction candidates.
Adaptive threshold relaxation: allowed in config but not applied (adaptive_relaxation_applied = false).
Operational/data risk: holdings refresh completed at startup; confidence scores ≈0.8 for universe names; no stale or missing holdings detected in this watchlist.

runs.objective: Refresh treasury holdings, compute mNAV snapshot, identify actionable treasury-arb pairs (paper), and produce a concrete 5-minute pre-market routine + risk-parameter review for tomorrow; post results to Discord.
runs.market_regime: Crypto-linked equities; elevated BTC volatility regime (recent 7d vol for names ~0.63–0.87 ann.), liquidity mixed across watchlist.
runs.analysis_summary: mNAV-driven treasury arbitrage with short-horizon momentum/mean-reversion overlay. mNAV = market_cap / estimated_NAV (NAV ≈ crypto holdings + cash - debt). Filtering on liquidity (20d avg volume), holdings confidence, and intraday crypto shocks.
trade_decisions: Premarket routine (5-minute focused run, target window 09:15–09:25 ET): 1) refresh holdings; 2) re-run mNAV snapshot; 3) run detect_mnav_arbitrage and produce top pairs. If mNAV still fails, construct NAV_per_share from holdings_value_usd / shares_outstanding and compute ad-hoc mNAV.
Entry rules (paper account): trade only names with avg USD ADV >= $750k, holdings confidence >= 0.75, and staleness_days == 0.
Preferred structure: dollar-neutral pairs (long discounted mNAV name vs short premium peer). Default paper sizing = $5,000 per leg. Max 2 new pairs per trading day.
Risk per leg: stop-loss = 8% from entry, take-profit = 15% or exit when mNAV reverts at least 50% toward peer/30d mean. If trade moves >+15% intraday, consider trimming 50% at +10% and trailing SL.
Position limits: max position per ticker = 2.5% of account equity (paper); max gross exposure = 10% of equity; net exposure target = 0 (dollar-neutral).
Volatility trigger: if BTC moves >3% intraday, reduce new trade size by 50% and tighten SL to 5%. Reassess live if crypto proxy correlation breaks.
Monitoring schedule: intraday checks at 11:30 ET and 15:30 ET. Auto-close or hedge pairs if system detects funding/liquidity event or market-wide gap/open >4% for the sector.
executed_orders: No market orders placed. (Paper execution deferred until mNAV pipeline validated.)
Discord status report posted with scan results and tomorrow plan.
risk_notes: Critical: data pipeline bug/mismatch for mNAV computation — market_cap units vs NAV units likely inconsistent. Fix is required before algorithmic sizing or live execution.
Liquidity: HUT's low ADV increases slippage risk — exclude unless manual approval and reduced sizing.
Sizing constraints: current default dollars_per_leg $5k is reasonable for paper but should scale to % of equity for live; enforce per-ticker cap (2.5%) and gross cap (10%).
Stop/TP asymmetric: 8% SL / 15% TP reasonable for mean-reversion pairs in this vol regime; consider tightening SL or reducing size when BTC moves >3%.
Operational risk: require holdings confidence >= 0.75 to trade; lower-confidence names (0.6) should be used only for research/backtest.

Strategy parameter suggestions to improve profitability: raise min_avg_dollar_volume from 500k → 750k; add holdings_confidence >=0.75 filter; consider reducing deviation_threshold from 15% → 12% after backtest validation to capture earlier mean reversion.
Execution risk: prefer paper orders / limit orders to reduce market impact; cap max_new_pairs/day to 2 to control overtrading.

runs.objective: Run a portfolio analysis, produce a 5-minute execution-ready plan for tomorrow, post the plan to Discord, and review/update risk parameters to improve profitability.
runs.market_regime: High crypto volatility / mean-reverting miner/treasury equities regime. BTC ~74.7k (elevated), short-term momentum signals muted; correlations across miner/treasury names remain high. Watch for crypto rallies >3% which would flip momentum overlays on.
runs.analysis_summary: mNAV-driven treasury-arbitrage + short-horizon momentum/mean-reversion overlay. mNAV = market_cap / estimated_NAV (NAV ≈ crypto holdings + cash - debt). Model requires canonical treasury holdings; currently data quality issues reduce actionable signal generation.
trade_decisions: No new market entries tomorrow while mNAV data remains incomplete — preserve capital and avoid blind exposure.
If BTC rallies >2% intraday: trim short MARA position by ~15–25% to reduce concentrated miner short risk (execute on strength).
If BTC/ETH rally >3% in premarket/first 30m: re-run scans and allow momentum overlay; consider small, dollar‑neutral pairs (target $5k–$10k per leg) only after mNAV confidence >= 70%.
Set intraday stop-losses for new positions: 6–8% hard stop; existing winners use trailing stops (10% trail).
Convert opportunistic ideas into small pairs once holdings updated and deviation >15% is confirmed.
executed_orders: Discord report posted with full analysis and action plan (2026-03-16 22:25 UTC).
risk_notes: Data hygiene priority: 6/10 watchlist names show missing treasury units (DFDV, BMNR, BTBT, CIFR, CORZ plus others flagged). Fix holdings file before enabling live mNAV trades.
Recommended risk parameter edits (to improve risk-adjusted profitability):
- missing_holdings_abort_threshold_pct: lower from 30% -> 20% (forces earlier abort/alert when holdings are missing). Rationale: prevents blind trading on incomplete NAV inputs.
- single_name_max: reduce from 20% -> 12% of portfolio equity. Rationale: reduces tail risk from concentrated miner/treasury names that can gap with crypto moves.
- portfolio_sector_max: reduce from 60% -> 40% to lower correlated crypto/miner concentration.
- risk_per_trade_var95: reduce from 1.0% -> 0.7% of equity to tighten sizing and improve Sharpe for small-bucket arb.
- max_new_trades_per_run: reduce from 4 -> 2 to limit simultaneous correlated entries.
- enable_llm_company_site_mnav_fallback: keep enabled but mark any LLM-inferred units as LOW_CONFIDENCE and exclude from live execution until confirmed by primary sources.
Stop-loss recommendations: 6–8% for new positions; trailing 10% for larger winners. Intraday monitoring required on crypto moves.
Operational recommendation: require >=70% of watchlist with verified holdings before running detect_mnav_arbitrage for live order placement.

runs.objective: Rebalance portfolio for tomorrow (market may be closed): reduce short exposure, keep core longs, and prepare GTC limit orders for next session; publish full analysis to Discord.
runs.market_regime: Crypto-treasury correlated regime — BTC ~74.7k; low dispersion signals from mNAV model today (neutral); prefer risk reduction ahead of potential crypto-driven moves.
runs.analysis_summary: Hybrid mNAV-driven crypto-treasury arbitrage + short-horizon momentum/mean-reversion overlay. Primary mNAV metric = market_cap / estimated_NAV (NAV ≈ crypto holdings + cash - debt). Model returned HOLD across the live treasury watchlist; decision prioritized portfolio risk reduction rather than initiating new directional, leveraged trades.
trade_decisions: COVER MARA: Buy 135 shares (partial cover of existing -335 short → target -200). Limit price: $9.25. Time-in-force: GTC. Est. cost ≈ $1,250–$1,300. Rationale: take profit / reduce directional exposure; liquidity OK.

COVER COIN: Buy 16 shares (close small short). Limit price: $210.50. Time-in-force: GTC. Est. cost ≈ $3,368. Rationale: remove a small short with unrealized loss; reduces correlation to crypto-treasury basket.

ADD MSTR: Buy 10 shares (scale into core long). Limit price: $150.00. Time-in-force: GTC. Est. cost ≈ $1,500. Rationale: modest scale at slight price improvement; keep size small relative to equity and liquidity constraints.
executed_orders: No limit or market orders were successfully placed via broker API. Multiple attempts to submit limit orders failed because the broker_submit_order_tool wrapper did not accept a limit_price parameter. No fills were executed by me.
Discord report of the rebalance plan was posted successfully.
risk_notes: Per-trade risk cap used: 2% of equity (~$1,957). Planned orders respect this cap (estimated cost per order < cap).
Max new trades allowed today: 3 (we prepared 3 limit orders).
Stop guidance: for new MSTR leg recommend protective stop at -8% from fill (or use a volatility-adjusted trailing stop) — re-evaluate after fill.
If MARA or COIN fills and crypto beta rises materially, consider (a) hedging via BTC proxy or (b) running auto-hedge (place_auto_hedge) — confirm if you want automatic hedge placement.

Data quality: treasury holdings up-to-date (staleness 1 day). mNAV values for many names are numerically extreme (NAV >> market cap) due to very large holdings value vs market cap estimates; model used gates and returned HOLD.

Operational constraint: Alpaca submit-order wrapper requires a limit_price input that is not currently accepted by the tool. I will not place market orders while market is closed or without explicit permission.
